item.title: 2016 in review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
A re짯view of the even짯ts that took pla짯ce this yea짯r, re짯lated to so짯ftwa짯re en짯gi짯nee짯ring and ar짯chi짯tec짯tu짯re.

The conferences, talks, and events

The짯se are so짯me of the even짯ts I par짯ti짯ci짯pated in:

  • (2 Apr 2016) PRO짯TO짯SEC 2016 1: A te짯ch짯ni짯cal con짯fe짯ren짯ce about ne짯two짯rkin짯g, 짯pro짯to짯col짯s, se짯cu짯ri짯ty, and re짯lated to짯pi짯cs.

  • (Ju짯짯ly 2016) Eu짯짯ro짯짯짯P짯짯y짯짯thon 2016: I atten짯짯ded se짯짯ve짯짯ral ta짯짯lks, tra짯i짯짯nings, wo짯짯짯rks짯ho짯p짯s, and pre짯sen짯ted a ta짯짯lk.

  • (10 Sep 2016) Py짯thon C처r짯do짯ba mee짯tup 6: in짯te짯res짯ting mee짯tup whe짯re I 짯pre짯sen짯ted my ta짯lk at Eu짯ro짯P짯y짯thon and atten짯ded the sprin짯ts afte짯rwar짯d짯s.

  • (14 Sep 2016) Ar짯q짯Conf Mee짯tup 쩔C처짯mo es tra짯ba짯jar dis짯tri짯bui짯do glo짯bal짯men짯te? 짯Ca짯so Open Sta짯ck 5: Hos짯ted in the Red Ha짯t's offi짯ces in Bue짯nos Ai짯res, this 짯ta짯lk was a ve짯ry in짯te짯res짯ting one, on whi짯ch I lear짯ned about the co짯lla짯bo짯ra짯ti짯ve way of wo짯rking of the Open Sta짯ck tea짯m, around the glo짯be.

  • (17 Nov 2016) Py짯짯thon Ba짯r짯짯ce짯짯lo짯짯짯na mee짯짯tup.

  • (9 Dec 2016) Ba짯r짯짯ce짯짯lo짯짯짯na Spa짯짯rk mee짯짯tup 2

  • (22 Dec 2016) Py짯thon Bar짯ce짯lo짯na De짯cem짯ber mee짯tup 7: I pre짯sen짯ted my ta짯lk a짯bout clean co짯de in Py짯thon (ye짯s, again :-), and the짯re we짯re two othe짯r in짯te짯res짯ting ta짯lks. Mi짯ne was the first one of the even짯in짯g. The se짯cond ta짯lk, was about zc.짯buil짯dout, an in짯te짯res짯ting tool for ma짯na짯ging pa짯cka짯ges an짯d en짯vi짯ron짯men짯ts in Py짯tho짯n, whi짯ch I first heard of at Py짯co짯nAr (P짯y짯thon Ar짯gen짯ti짯na grou짯p) 2012. The third and last ta짯lk, 짯pre짯sen짯ted a pro짯ject na짯med aio짯ca짯che 8: a ca짯che sys짯tem im짯ple짯men짯ted in as짯yn짯cio, that su짯ppor짯ts mul짯ti짯ple ba짯ck-en짯d짯s. The ta짯lk wa짯lked us th짯rou짯gh the 짯co짯de, its ar짯chi짯tec짯tu짯re, and so짯me use ca짯ses wi짯th de짯mo짯s.

Code wise

I started with a small contribution to the coala project 3, and during the EuroPython sprints I contributed to aiohttp with a pull request.

There were two new releases of my vim configuration, versions v0.3 and v0.4 4, which is great because is the single tool I use every day, and it is nice to have several improvements on it.

I al짯so enhan짯ced my dot fi짯les, bu짯t 짯that is sti짯ll in be짯ta.

On general

A minor release of Python3.5, but most importantly: Python3.6 was recently released!

There were two releases of Fedora, 24 and 25, and there were lots of improvements and changes in the Linux Kernel.

Regarding information security, there were some issues in the Linux Kernel, also in applications (such as dirty COW, etc.), and a massive DDoS attack that stroke the Internet on October, due to IoT devices being compromised.

What to look forward to for the new year

  • I sti짯짯ll look fo짯짯짯rward for mo짯짯짯re co짯n짯짯tri짯짯bu짯짯tions to open sou짯r짯짯ce, in pa짯r짯짯ti짯짯cu짯짯la짯짯r wi짯짯th CP짯짯y짯짯tho짯짯n.

  • Mo짯re func짯tio짯nal pro짯gra짯m짯ming (S짯ca짯la/Ha짯ske짯ll ma짯y짯be짯), lo짯w-짯le짯vel pro짯gra짯m짯ming in 짯C, and re짯sear짯ch in the field of in짯fo짯sec.

  • Keep on pa짯r짯짯ti짯짯ci짯짯pa짯짯ting on te짯짯ch짯짯ni짯짯cal co짯n짯짯fe짯짯ren짯짯ce짯s.

And a lot mo짯re.
